input.topic: Heartbleed zero-day canonical merge and legacy reference cleanup
input.notes: Corpus review confirmed that `heartbleed-openssl-tls-cve-2014-0160.md` duplicated the canonical Heartbleed zero-day entry for CVE-2014-0160. The duplicate article was retired in favor of `heartbleed-openssl-cve-2014-0160.md` and a legacy redirect was added. Follow up by checking for any downstream references, manifests, or validators that should explicitly enforce a single canonical zero-day page per CVE family.

history.note: Queued follow-on cleanup after corpus review collapsed duplicate Heartbleed zero-day coverage into a single canonical article with a legacy redirect.

downstream audit — zero in-scope refs to legacy heartbleed-openssl-tls-cve-2014-0160 slug remain; redirect in place; single-canonical-per-CVE validator enforcement deliberately not added in this slice (wider scope; leave as candidate for a later validator pass)
